behavior_summary: This script monitors a referenced item/actor's vertical position (Z) and, when the Z reaches or exceeds a threshold (0x70 / 112) and a global latch is not already set, triggers a "break wind" response: it sets a global flag, plays one of two SFX (randomized), and in one branch schedules a delayed retry via a spawned FREE timer.

The top-level function U8P_BRKWIND::hatch runs as a persistent process that periodically yields (suspend) and re-checks conditions rather than executing once. It reads a stack-local reference to determine which item/actor to inspect.

U8P_BRKWIND::func00CA contains the branch logic: it re-checks Item::getZ on the same referenced item, writes the breakWind global flag based on the threshold test, chooses between two sound effects using urandom, and on one branch computes a random delay (temp = urandom(0x0096) + 0x78) and spawns FREE::func1C19 as a delayed callback (capturing the pid).
notable_control_flow: Long-running process pattern: U8P_BRKWIND::hatch initializes minimal local state, then repeatedly suspend/resume to poll conditions instead of blocking.
Guarded trigger: U8P_BRKWIND::hatch reads global flag breakWind; if breakWind is true it will not re-run the break behavior, preventing repeated triggers.
Threshold check: the core trigger condition is Item::getZ([stack-ref]) >= 0x70 (112).
Helper-driven branching: U8P_BRKWIND::func00CA decides SFX and whether to schedule a delayed spawn; uses urandom to randomize both SFX selection and delay length.
open_questions: What is the precise game-world meaning of the breakWind flag (naming suggests a one-time environmental change, but exact gameplay effect is not present in the snippet)?
What exact item/actor is referenced by the stack-local pointer ([SP-00]) used by Item::getZ (the script relies on this implicit reference rather than a named parameter)?
What does the spawned FREE::func1C19 callback do when run (we only see the spawn pattern and not the callback body), and how long is the intended retry/re-trigger period in gameplay terms?
Are there other systems that set or clear breakWind externally which would affect the observed behavior (race conditions with other scripts)?

main_function.name: U8P_BRKWIND::hatch
main_function.purpose: Persistent monitor that periodically checks a referenced item's Z coordinate and, if the global guard 'breakWind' is not set and Z >= 0x70, invokes helper logic to perform the "break wind" response (set flag, play SFX, optionally schedule a delayed callback).
entry_conditions: Process starts on spawn/activation of the associated item/object (isProcess = true).
Implicit input: the function uses a stack-local reference ([SP-00]) to identify which item/actor to inspect for Z.
Runs repeatedly, yielding between checks via suspend to create a polling interval.

function_map.name: U8P_BRKWIND::hatch
function_map.role: Persistent process that polls an item/actor Z coordinate and conditionally triggers the helper to 'break wind'.

function_map.name: U8P_BRKWIND::func00CA
function_map.role: Performs the threshold test, writes the global breakWind flag, plays one of two SFX based on urandom, and optionally schedules a delayed spawn for follow-up behavior.

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Read the vertical coordinate (Z) of the referenced item/actor used as the trigger condition.

function_map.name: FREE::func1C19
function_map.role: Target of spawn used as a one-off/delayed callback; exact body unknown but used as a timer scheduler.

internal_calls.purpose: When breakWind is not set and the monitored item Z >= 0x70, delegate handling (flag write, SFX, optional delayed spawn) to the helper.

external_calls.purpose: Read the referenced item/actor's vertical position to decide whether to call the helper (threshold check).
Re-check the Z coordinate before setting/clearing breakWind and selecting behavior.
Generate random values used to (1) choose between two SFX, and (2) compute a randomized delay added to a base (temp = urandom(0x0096) + 0x78) for spawn scheduling.
Play one of two sound effects (IDs 0x73 or 0x74) to signal the wind-break attempt/result.
Schedule a delayed, one-off FREE callback using a computed delay (temp) and capture the returned pid for bookkeeping.
Yield the monitoring process between checks to implement periodic polling rather than tight looping.

reads.name: breakWind
reads.likely_effect: Used as a guard to avoid re-triggering the break behavior if already set (prevents repeated SFX/spawn actions).

writes.name: breakWind
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the referenced item's Z >= 0x70 (indicating the 'wind' has been broken); cleared otherwise. Serves as a latch to suppress further triggers.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script monitors a referenced item/actor's vertical position (Z) and, when the Z reaches or exceeds a threshold (0x70 / 112) and a global latch is not already set, triggers a "break wind" response: it sets a global flag, plays one of two SFX (randomized), and in one branch schedules a delayed retry via a spawned FREE timer.
